title: Access The RMS Web Application And Reach The Overview Screen
summary: Open the site RMS web application using the site-specific address associated with the site's Argus server, log in with username and password, and confirm the RMS overview screen is displayed. The training segment presents this as a support-facing starting point for learning more about a system issue.
when_to_use: Use when support personnel need to access a site's RMS web application and verify they have reached the RMS overview screen as the starting point for investigating or learning more about a system issue.
not_for: Do not use for deeper RMS navigation or troubleshooting actions not shown in this source segment.
Do not use when the site-specific RMS web location or unique IP address is not known.
Do not use when valid RMS username and password are not available.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Access the site RMS web location, log in with provided credentials, and verify the overview screen is displayed.

safety_notes: The source frames this as a support-facing access and viewing procedure only.
The source does not provide authorization to bypass OptiSweep or perform direct control changes in RMS.
The source notes risk of systems getting out of sync if OptiSweep is bypassed; this runbook is limited to access and overview confirmation.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the site's RMS web location or unique IP address
Web browser access to the RMS web application

access_required: Site-specific RMS web location or unique IP address
system_preconditions: The site has an Argus server providing access to the RMS web application.

visual_references.description: Training video frame showing the RMS web application context and the overview screen referenced in the segment.

steps.title: Obtain the site RMS web address
steps.purpose: Identify the correct site-specific RMS access point before attempting login.
steps.instruction: Obtain the site-specific RMS web location or unique IP address associated with the site's Argus server.
steps.expected_result: A valid RMS web location or unique IP address for the site is available.
steps.healthy_condition: The support user has the site-specific RMS web location or unique IP address needed to access the RMS service.
steps.failure_condition: The site-specific RMS web location or unique IP address is unknown or unavailable.

actions.label: Identify RMS address
actions.exact_action: Obtain the site-specific RMS web location or unique IP address associated with the site's Argus server.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Reference that each site has an Argus server and RMS is accessed through a web location or unique IP address.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop and escalate if the site-specific RMS address or unique IP address is not known.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: For each site, we're gonna have a Argus server and you're gonna access the RMS web location... it's the RMS web application and you're gonna have a unique IP address to access this RMS service.

steps.title: Open the RMS web application
steps.purpose: Reach the RMS login page using the site-specific address.
steps.instruction: Open the RMS web application at the site-specific address or unique IP address.
steps.expected_result: The RMS web application is opened and ready for login.
steps.healthy_condition: The RMS web application is reachable from the provided address.
steps.failure_condition: The RMS web application does not open from the provided address.

actions.label: Open RMS address
actions.exact_action: Open the RMS web application at that address.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The training frame and transcript context describing access to the RMS web application through the site-specific address.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop and escalate if the RMS web application cannot be reached from the provided site-specific address.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: you're gonna access the RMS web location... it's the RMS web application and you're gonna have a unique IP address to access this RMS service.

steps.title: Log in with username and password
steps.purpose: Authenticate into the RMS web application.
steps.instruction: Enter the provided username and password to log in.
steps.expected_result: The user is authenticated into RMS.
steps.healthy_condition: The provided username and password are accepted by the RMS web application.
steps.failure_condition: The username and password are unavailable or are not accepted.

actions.label: Enter credentials
actions.exact_action: Enter the provided username and password to log in.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The segment context stating that after accessing the address, the user enters username and password.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop and escalate if valid username and password are not available.
Stop and escalate if the provided credentials are not accepted.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Once you access that address, you put in the username and password...

steps.title: Confirm the overview screen is displayed
steps.purpose: Verify that login succeeded and the expected RMS landing screen is visible.
steps.instruction: Confirm that the RMS overview screen appears after login.
steps.expected_result: The RMS overview screen is visible.
steps.healthy_condition: The screen shown after login matches the overview screen described in the training segment.
steps.failure_condition: The expected overview screen does not appear after login.

actions.label: Verify overview screen
actions.exact_action: Confirm that the RMS overview screen appears after login.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The RMS web application overview screen shown in the training video frame, including the overview area mentioned in the transcript.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the expected overview screen does not appear after login.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: And this is what you will see. This is the overview screen.

steps.title: Use the overview screen as the starting point
steps.purpose: Establish the overview screen as the initial support view for learning more about the system issue.
steps.instruction: Use the overview screen as the starting point for learning more information about the system issue, as described in the training segment.
steps.expected_result: The user is positioned at the RMS overview screen and can begin reviewing system information from that starting point.
steps.healthy_condition: The overview screen is available as the initial support workspace for further issue review.
steps.failure_condition: The overview screen is not available, preventing the user from starting from the expected RMS view.

actions.label: Begin from overview
actions.exact_action: Use the overview screen as the starting point for learning more information about the system issue.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The overview screen context presented as the support starting point for learning more about system behavior or issues.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the overview screen cannot be reached or confirmed.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The segment is framed for support use: what the RMS overview screen looks like, what operators can expect when opening it, and how they can use it to investigate problems and learn more about ATP behavior.

success_criteria: The site-specific RMS web location or unique IP address is obtained.
The RMS web application opens from that address.
The user logs in with username and password.
The RMS overview screen is displayed after login.
healthy_conditions: A valid site-specific RMS address is available.
The RMS web application is reachable.
The overview screen appears after login.
failure_conditions: The site-specific RMS address or unique IP address is not known.
Valid username and password are not available.
The RMS web application cannot be reached.
The expected overview screen does not appear after login.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the site-specific RMS address or unique IP address is not known.
Escalate if valid username and password are not available.
Escalate if the expected overview screen does not appear after login.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: RMS web application context and overview screen shown in the training segment.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: For each site, we're gonna have a Argus server and you're gonna access the RMS web location... it's the RMS web application and you're gonna have a unique IP address to access this RMS service.
Once you access that address, you put in the username and password... And this is what you will see. This is the overview screen.

missing_details: The source does not provide the actual RMS URL, hostname, or IP address format.
The source does not provide example usernames or password handling guidance.
The source does not define what specific overview screen fields must be checked beyond confirming the overview screen is displayed.
The source does not provide detailed next-step navigation from the overview screen.
